full_prompt: Design a NATO 463L pallet-compatible cargo tie-down bracket per a representative NATO spare-mechanical-parts RFP, using AISI 4130 normalized steel, meeting MIL-HDBK-1791 air transportability and MIL-A-8625 ground-handling requirements, rated for 10,000 lbf (44.5 kN) working load with 3:1 SF on ultimate breaking load per NATO STANAG 4062 cargo restraint. Envelope: 100 x 70 x 35 mm. Interfaces: two through-holes on pallet side for 3/8-16 UNC grade 8 bolts on 60 mm spacing; a 20 mm oblong tie-off slot for standard MB-2 tie-down strap hook on accessory side. Material: AISI 4130 normalized (yield 460 MPa, UTS 670 MPa, E 205 GPa, density 7850 kg/m^3); plating: zinc-nickel per ASTM B841 (10 um minimum) or cadmium per AMS-QQ-P-416 Type II Class 2. Load cases per MIL-HDBK-1791 + STANAG 4062: LC1 working load 44,500 N along tie-down strap axis (pulled upward at 30 degrees from horizontal representing typical cargo net angle); LC2 ultimate test load 133,500 N (3x working, per STANAG 4062 breaking test); LC3 angled pull 30-45 degrees off nominal strap axis at 44,500 N; LC4 10 g crash load (MIL-STD-1791 restraint design) applied as 10 x cargo lumped mass. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises at working load LC1 <= 460 / 1.5 = 307 MPa (SF 1.5 on yield at working load per ground-handling practice); (R2) max von Mises at ultimate test LC2 <= UTS = 670 MPa (no fracture at 3x working, per STANAG 4062); (R3) bracket must not permanently deform (von Mises < yield) at any point under 1.5x working load 66,750 N (service-limit overload test); (R4) pallet-side bolt bearing stress <= 0.9 * yield = 414 MPa at LC1; (R5) edge distance from strap slot edge to tie-off face >= 2 * strap width / thickness ratio (design-guide edge rule, practically 15 mm minimum); (R6) mass <= 300 g. Deliverables: STEP AP242, NATO-compatible DI-CMAN-80008B drawing pack, material certification to ASTM A29/A29M, plating certification per ASTM B841 or AMS-QQ-P-416; FEA + hand-calculation report showing working-load, overload, and ultimate test case results.
short_prompt:
Design a NATO 463L pallet-compatible cargo tie-down bracket per a representative NATO spare-mechanical-parts RFP, using AISI 4130 normalized steel, meeting MIL-HDBK-1791 air transportability and MIL-A-8625 ground-handling requirements, rated for 10,000 lbf (44.5 kN) working load with 3:1 SF on ultimate breaking load per NATO STANAG 4062.

Envelope: 100 x 70 x 35 mm. Interfaces: two through-holes on pallet side for 3/8-16 UNC grade 8 bolts on 60 mm spacing; a 20 mm oblong tie-off slot for standard MB-2 tie-down strap hook.

Material: AISI 4130 normalized; plating zinc-nickel per ASTM B841 (>= 10 um) or cadmium per AMS-QQ-P-416 Type II Class 2.

Load cases per MIL-HDBK-1791 + STANAG 4062.
- LC1 working load 44,500 N along the tie-down strap axis (30 degrees from horizontal).
- LC2 ultimate test 133,500 N (3x working, STANAG 4062 breaking test).
- LC3 angled pull 30-45 degrees off nominal at 44,500 N.
- LC4 10 g crash load (MIL-STD-1791 restraint design) on cargo lumped mass.

Pass criteria include no permanent deformation under 1.5x working load 66,750 N; bolt bearing <= 0.9 * yield at LC1; strap-slot edge distance >= 15 mm; mass <= 300 g.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: STANAG 4062 breaking test is a physical test, not FEA.
examples: tensile test rig per ASTM E8

prompt.title: NATO 463L-compatible 10,000 lbf cargo tie-down bracket (NATO spare-mechanical-parts RFP template)
prompt.brief: Design a 100x70x35 mm 4130 tie-down bracket, 10,000 lbf working load with 3:1 SF per STANAG 4062.

geometric_constraints.plating: zinc-nickel ASTM B841 or cadmium AMS-QQ-P-416 Type II Class 2

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: UTS (no fracture at 3x working per STANAG 4062)

pass_fail_criteria.note: LC1 magnitude multiplied by 1.5 in this check only.

requirements.governing_standard: STANAG 4062 + MIL-HDBK-1791 + MIL-A-8625 + ASTM A29/A29M + ASTM B841 or AMS-QQ-P-416

notes.numeric_source: STANAG 4062 cargo restraint 3:1 SF; representative 10,000 lbf working-load NATO 463L tie-down rating (typical).
